evals.prompt: I want to check my open GitHub PRs every weekday morning around 9am and append a brief summary to ~/Desktop/pr-log.txt. Set it up.
evals.expected_output: Guides user through CREATE flow: drafts a self-contained prompt for the job, sets weekday cron expression in local time (e.g. 57 8 * * 1-5), confirms recurring=true, asks about durable, shows config, calls CronCreate, reports job ID and 7-day expiry warning.

evals.prompt: Remind me in 45 minutes to review the staging deployment before it gets promoted to prod.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as a one-shot reminder. Drafts a prompt with the reminder message, calculates a one-time cron expression ~45 minutes from now, sets recurring=false, skips durable question (in-memory is fine for a reminder), shows config, calls CronCreate.

evals.prompt: What jobs do I have scheduled right now?
evals.expected_output: Calls CronList and displays results in readable format: human-readable schedule, next fire time, durable flag, and job ID for each active job.
